summarization.a: The figure shows the intensity of N 1s peaks for different plasma powers and temperatures.
summarization.b: The figure illustrates the conductivity (Cond) of LiPO and LiPON materials as a function of inverse temperature (1000/T).

a.question: What plasma power and temperatures were used in the experiments?
a.answer: 275 °C and 100 °C, and plasma power of 100 W, 200 W and 300 W.

a.question: Which sample exhibits a larger amount of species in the N 1s peak?
a.answer: The 100 °C, 100 W sample exhibits more intense peaks, as well as a shoulder related to the presence of other N species.

b.question: How does conductivity change when the 1000/T value increases?
b.answer: Conductivity decreases for all the samples, as the 1000/T value increases.

b.question: How does conductivity change when N2 plasma is added?
b.answer: Adding N2 plasma increases the conductivity of the samples.
